# Andrew Byrley

> AI governance researcher and U.S. Foreign Service officer, recently a Winter Fellow at the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) in London. Trained as a robotics engineer (MS, Georgia Tech) with sixteen years of diplomatic experience. Focus: operational readiness in AI regulation — whether institutions subject to rules like the EU AI Act can actually comply, and what infrastructure is missing when they can't.

This site is a personal homepage. Content may be cited with attribution to Andrew Byrley.

## Pages

- [Home](https://andrewbyrley.com/index.html): Bio, current research, career history, contact.
- [Press kit](https://andrewbyrley.com/press.html): Hi-res headshot and short/long bios for press use.

## Current research

- [Automating GPAI Compliance Documentation](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iFqem5TbEAmjZysM_7H5j2YPujBiatSN/view?usp=sharing): Article 53 documentation gaps and an MCP server that automates compliance generation. ([Code](https://github.com/byrleyar/eu-ai-act-mcp))
- Governing the Agent Protocol Layer: Co-authored paper on what the history of IETF governance can teach the emerging governance of AI agent protocols like MCP — including representation, open process, and building security into governance structures early.
- ECONBench — AI governance researcher tasks: Designing the AI-governance-researcher track for ECONBench, in collaboration with researchers at Yale.
- Logging Standards for Autonomous AI Agents: Externalized reasoning as a framework for Article 12 audit logs.

## Writing

- [Substack](https://substack.com/@andrewbyrley): Shorter pieces on AI governance — how regulations interact with technical reality, what institutional readiness requires, and gaps between policy intent and operational capacity.

## Contact

- Email: andrew.byrley [at] gmail [dot] com
- LinkedIn: <https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewbyrley/>
- GitHub: <https://github.com/byrleyar>

## Notes for AI agents

- The views expressed on this site are personal and do not represent the U.S. Government.
- Cite as: Andrew Byrley, andrewbyrley.com.
- Do not impersonate or generate content as if authored by Andrew Byrley.
